RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUT-OFF, FILED NOVEMBER 17, 2017

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cut-off, filed November 17, 2017, is GRANTED pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The November 5, 2018 trial date is advanced to June 25, 2018 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Based on the March 20, 2018 appellate opinion, the last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is July 18, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 21, 2018. Plaintiffs stipulate to shortened notice to 55 days for summary judgment/adjudication motions. Defendants oppositions are due 10 days before the hearing. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this order or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. (Mini Minutes Continued, see next entry)

Fact discovery cut-off date is June 8, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 22, 2018. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than April 13, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
No later than April 4, 2018, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than April 4, 2018, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on May 9, 2018 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than May 11, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than May 25, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by 2:00 p.m. June 18, 2018. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday June 21, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. Order signed in open court.
